findings.title: Excessive Permission Surface
findings.recommendation: Tool requests broad permissions (exec/fs/network). Validate input parameters using Enums where possible, and restrict file system operations to explicit allowed directories.

findings.title: DoS Resilience — Missing Rate Limit / Timeout
findings.description: tool performs network or execution operations but declares no rate-limit, timeout, or retry configuration
findings.recommendation: Declare explicit rate-limit, timeout, and retry configuration for all network and execution tools. Implement exponential back-off and surface resource state to the calling agent.

findings.title: Arbitrary Code Execution
findings.description: tool name or description implies arbitrary script/code execution (evaluate_script, execute javascript, etc.)
findings.recommendation: This tool can execute arbitrary code or shell commands on the host system. Remove it unless strictly required. If kept: (1) restrict access to trusted users/agents only, (2) require human approval before each invocation (Claude Desktop: set approval_required: true; other clients: enable equivalent confirmation), (3) use the most restrictive sandbox or read-only mode available, and (4) never expose this tool to untrusted input sources.
